calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-25-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carole Scagnetti & Joseph Urbanski for (administrator) Frances Ducey who is appearing via court call.

The Court stated that this is an amended petition for final distribution and asked counsel why was an agreement provided for $29,704.83 in taxes and fees yet $32,418.92 was paid. Ms. Scagnetti stated that the number negotiated was based on information that they had at the time, that the decedent was behind in taxes, that the fees were offset by refunds and that there is a difference of $713.00. The Court stated that the agreement did not say it will pay additional amounts. Mr. Urbanski stated that the estate was willing to take it on and that it did not want to go back to the ex Mrs. Ducey. The Court stated that having reached an agreement where does the authority come from to pay more. Ms. Scagnetti stated that there was an additional amount due to penalties and interest, that it was due to the decedent's failure to file taxes, that the amount was negotiated down and that it is hard to negotiate with the ex Mrs. Ducey. The Court stated that it does not answer the question, that it was negotiated at a certain amount and that it could have been subject to a final calculation. Ms. Scagnetti stated that the additional amount was not tax, it was penalties and interest. The Court turned to extraordinary fees stating that they spent a lot of time given the values in the estate, that there are charges at the attorney rate for administrative work and that the billing people are either not qualified or qualified at a lower rate. Ms. Scagnetti clarified the status of those billing and explained the context of when they got the estate, that it was sudden, under emotional circumstances, that the marital settlement was very heated, that the medical office was in disarray, that he was the main tenant at the practice location with sub tenants, that Mrs. Ducey could have hired other people but it was hard for her to get here, that there were tenants in the condo and that there was some coordination done by their office. Mr. Urbanski reported that the judgment has been submitted in the family law matter. The Court took the accounting petition under submission stating that the fees are likely to be reduced.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-05-2016 Continued To May-10-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jul-15-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carole Scagnetti for Frances Ducey.

Ms. Scagnetti stated that they have found nothing that brings further clarification, that they are unclear on what the duty of the personal representative is in regards to the medical records, that the practice needs to be disposed of, that the practice had deteriorated to the point of no clients, that they have been unable to get someone to take it over and are concerned regarding what it will cost the personal representative to maintain the records. The petition for instructions was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Park. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-29-2015 Continued To Jul-06-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
